BusinessName.BusinessNameLine1Txt: KENTUCKY CENTER FOR THE ARTS FOUNDATION INC

IRS990.ActivityOrMissionDesc: Solicitation and receipt of gifts grants and contributions from individuals groups corporations and other sources public and private to finance the capital operating and programming costs of The Kentucky Center for the Arts Corporation.
IRS990.MissionDesc: Solicitation and receipt of gifts grants and contributions from individuals groups corporations and other sources public and private to finance the capital operating and programming costs of The Kentucky Center for the Arts Corporation.
IRS990.Desc: Operating support for The Kentucky Center for the Arts Corporation.

ProgSrvcAccomActy2Grp.Desc: Lease agreement with The Kentucky Center for the Arts Corporation for the operation of the Brown Theatre theatrical venue.

ProgSrvcAccomActy3Grp.Desc: Support for education program - Kentucky Governors School for the Arts - 512 students attended a 3-week in-person intensive arts education program. Students also participated in related workshops throughout the year.

ProgSrvcAccomActyOtherGrp.Desc: Support for education program - ArtsReach and underserved youth.
Parking garage repairs & maintenance and depreciation.
Government relations to monitor legislative and budget issues that may affect The Kentucky Center for the Arts Corporation.

Form990ScheduleAPartVIGrp.ExplanationTxt: | Year:, Amount:, Description:| 2019, 0, | 2020, 44483debt forgiveness| 2021, 54proceeds from class action lawsuit related to securities held| 2022, 670proceeds from class action lawsuit related to securities held| 2023, 13940other revenues|

SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: | PartandLinenumber:,Explanation:| Part2SectionBLine1i,An unrelated organization is paid a monthly stipend to monitor and inform of legislation budget issues affecting The Kentucky Center for the Arts Corporation.|

| PartandLinenumber:,Explanation:| Part III Line 4,The Kentucky Center for the Arts Foundation Inc. owns a diverse array of contemporary art which is on permanent display throughout The Kentucky Center for the Arts and includes work by artists such as John Chamberlain Malcolm Morley Alexander Calder John DuBuffet Louise Nevelson and John Miro.| Part V Line 4,The Kentucky Center for the Arts Foundation Inc uses its endowment funds to make grants to the Kentucky Center for the Arts Corporation to support the organizations operating programming and capital cost.|

RecipientBusinessName.BusinessNameLine1Txt: Kentucky Center for the Arts Coporation

SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: The Kentucky Center for the Arts Foundation Inc. "Foundation" supports the operating programming and capital costs of The Kentucky Center for the Arts Corporations "Corporation". The Foundation budgets annual grants from endowment funds per the spending policy of the Foundation. The budget is approved by the Foundation Board of Directors. The Executive Committee of the Corporation and the Foundation meet jointly once a quarter. The Board of Directors of the Corporation and Foundation meet jointly twice a year. Directors of both organizations participate in strategic planning periodically.

Form 990 is prepared by the Director of Accounting. It is reviewed by the organizations CPA firm and the Treasurer of The Kentucky Center for the Arts Foundation Inc. for approval. It is then distributed to board members.

All Directors are required to sign a conflict of interest statement to disclose any conflicts. All documents are reviewed by the Governance Committee. All disclosures are noted in the minutes of the board meeting.
Documents are maintained at 501 West Main Street Louisville Kentucky 40202 and are available upon request.
